PR surface: Source +17, Tests +10. Total +27 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR is a performance cleanup, and current main's source shows the repeated array-copy and linear-scan paths without needing a failing user repro.

target.title: perf(browser): index role snapshot references
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Browser role snapshots repeatedly allocate duplicate-reference arrays and scan the full accessibility tree to find a referenced node. Compact snapshots also rescan descendants for every branch, which can become quadratic on large trees.

## Why This Change Was Made

The ref bookkeeping now mutates one array per duplicate key and keeps a direct ref-to-node map. `compactTree` uses one stack-based pass to retain ref-bearing ancestors without rescanning descendants.

## User Impact

Large browser snapshots use less CPU and allocation pressure, with no output or reference-resolution behavior change.

## Evidence

- Focused tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-role-snapshot.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.snapshot.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.internal.test.ts`
- Result: 48 tests passed.
- Fresh autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Rebased source commit: `2b13c25f94`
- The earlier hosted CI failure was unrelated baseline drift in `test-projects`; this push is rebased onto the current `origin/main`.
